verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein (large N-terminal ectodomain, single TM helix, short cytoplasmic tail) that reaches the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane by its own TM anchor. However, surface exposure is strictly cell-state conditioned: in fresh/uncapacitated sperm IZUMO1 is sequestered inside the acrosome and is not detectable on the cell surface. Only after the acrosome reaction (triggered during capacitation in the female tract) does IZUMO1 translocate to the sperm plasma membrane surface, where it mediates JUNO binding and gamete fusion. This documented, state-dependent surface exposure qualifies as contextual / cell_state_induced. The protein is also sperm-specific at the protein level, adding further tissue restriction.
